title: macledium ellipticum
intro: Macledium ellipticum, commonly known as the Yellow Balsam, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. This plant is valued for its health benefits, which include the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds due to its expectorant properties. The therapeutic actions of Macledium ellipticum include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its use in treating various ailments. Bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenes are present in the plant, contributing to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Macledium ellipticum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to its high alkaloid content. Prolonged use or excessive consumption can lead to kidney damage and liver toxicity. Some individuals may also experience skin irritation, allergic reactions, or increased heart rate after ingesting the plant. In severe cases, improper use of Macledium ellipticum can cause seizures, coma, or even death.
